chunks.text: Alvin Cheung is a Professor in EECS at UC Berkeley. His contact information includes 785 Soda Hall and email akcheung@cs.berkeley.edu. Research areas include Database Management Systems (DBMS) and Programming Systems (PS). He is affiliated with research centers EPIC Data lab, Sky Computing Lab, and SpeciaLIzed Computing Ecosystems (SLICE).
chunks.question: What are Alvin Cheung's research areas?
chunks.answer: Database Management Systems and Programming Systems.

chunks.text: Alvin Cheung teaches CS 186: Introduction to Database Systems, scheduled for MoWe 10:00-11:29 in Soda 306. Selected publications include 'LLM-Based Code Translation Needs Formal Compositional Reasoning' (2025) and 'PlotCoder: Hierarchical Decoding for Synthesizing Visualization Code in Programmatic Context' (2021).
chunks.question: What course does Alvin Cheung teach?

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards include ACSIC Rock Star Award (2025), AITO Dahl Nygaard Junior Prize (2024), VLDB Early Career Award (2023), Army Research Office Young Investigator Award (2022), ACM SIGPLAN Distinguished Paper Award (2022), Office of Naval Research Young Investigator (2021), ACM SIGCHI Best Paper Award (2021), Intel Outstanding Researcher Award (2020), IEEE CS TCDE Early Career Award (2020), VMware Early Career Faculty Award (2020), DOE PECASE Award (2019), ACM SIGSOFT Distinguished Paper Award (2019), Sloan Research Fellow (2019), Google Faculty Research Award (2018), ACM SIGMOD Best Demonstration Award (2017), DOE Early Career Research Award (2016), NSF CAREER Award (2016).
chunks.question: What major award did Alvin Cheung receive in 2019?
